body.title: CLOSED: /sprint-loop autonomous queue drain skill
body.result: sprint-loop skill created in agent_datacenter + deployed to ~/.claude/skills/sprint-loop
body.description:
When a full queue is ready to sprint, manual nudging between batches is the bottleneck. /sprint-batch sprints all current tickets then /autocompact fires compact — but after compact, someone has to say 'keep going.' With a design day filling the queue, this becomes painful.

Create ~/.claude/skills/sprint-loop skill. Core loop:
1. Check queue for pending items (cc_queue.py list, filter status=triage or sprint)
2. If empty → print 'queue drained — sprint-loop complete' → do NOT schedule wakeup (terminates)
3. If items → ScheduleWakeup(delaySeconds=90, prompt='/sprint-loop [selector]', reason='continuing queue drain') FIRST — before sprinting, so compact mid-sprint does not lose the loop
4. Invoke /sprint-batch [selector] for all currently pending items
5. /autocompact fires naturally at batch end; when CC wakes from the scheduled wakeup, back to step 1

Accepts the same selectors as /sprint-batch (default: today-slate; also decision:D-..., tag:..., explicit ids). The 90s delay is shorter than a typical ticket sprint — the wakeup queues up and fires when CC is next idle after compact.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/sprint-loop (new skill file)
**Design rules:** schedule wakeup before batch (not after) — survivable through compact; termination is queue-empty (no wakeup scheduled); selector must be passed through to /sprint-batch
**Scope boundary:** In: skill file, ScheduleWakeup loop, selector passthrough, empty-queue termination. Out: modifying /sprint-batch or /autocompact internals.
**Test plan:** /sprint-loop with empty queue → no wakeup scheduled, prints 'queue drained'. /sprint-loop with 1 pending ticket → wakeup scheduled, ticket sprinted. Selector argument passed through to /sprint-batch correctly.
